title: National Fuel Increases Dividend Rate for 56th Consecutive Year
author: Na
description: National Fuel Gas Company (NYSE: NFG) announced a 4% increase in its quarterly dividend from 53.5 cents to 55.5 cents per share, marking the 56th consecutive year of dividend increases. The new dividend will be paid on July 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 30, 2026. The company has maintained dividend payments for 124 consecutive years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company demonstrated strong financial health and commitment to shareholders by increasing its dividend for the 56th consecutive year. This consistent track record of dividend growth and 124 years of uninterrupted dividend payments indicate financial stability and investor confidence, which are positive indicators for the company's long-term prospects.

title: National Fuel Declares Quarterly Dividend and Reports Preliminary Voting Results from the Annual Meeting of Stockholders
author: Globe Newswire
description: National Fuel Gas Company's Board of Directors approved a regular quarterly dividend of 53.5 cents per share, payable April 15, 2026. At its Annual Meeting of Stockholders, the company announced that all three management proposals were approved, including the election of 11 directors for one-year terms, an advisory vote on executive compensation, and the ratification of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the independent auditor.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a quarterly dividend of 53.5 cents per share and successfully obtained stockholder approval for all three management proposals, including board elections and auditor ratification. These actions demonstrate shareholder confidence and operational stability.

title: Want Decades of Passive Income? 3 Energy Stocks to Buy Right Now
author: Courtney Carlsen
description: The article recommends three energy dividend stocks for passive income: ExxonMobil (43 consecutive years of dividend growth), Enbridge (31 consecutive years with a fee-based model less affected by commodity prices), and National Fuel Gas (55 consecutive years of dividend growth, achieving Dividend King status). These companies offer stable cash flows and inflation protection through their diversified energy portfolios.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 43 consecutive years of dividend growth, integrated business model providing resilience, strategic shift to low-cost advantaged assets, strong capital management, and technological investments for efficient operations.

31 consecutive years of dividend growth, fee-based model provides stable cash flows independent of commodity prices, operates largest crude oil and liquids network, expanding gas infrastructure for data center demand.

55 consecutive years of dividend growth (Dividend King status), diversified natural gas company with regulated utility segment providing stable predictable cash flow, strong competitive position across natural gas value chain.

title: What a $3 Million Add to National Fuel Gas Stock Signals to Long-Term Investors
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: GAMCO Investors added 37,056 shares of National Fuel Gas Company (NFG) in Q4 2025, valued at approximately $3.05 million, bringing their total position to $115.73 million. Despite the position value declining $14.37 million quarter-over-quarter, the purchase signals conviction in the company. National Fuel Gas demonstrated strong operational performance with adjusted earnings up 24% year-over-year and production rising 12%, supported by its vertically integrated business model spanning exploration, production, transportation, and regulated utility services.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong operational metrics including 24% YoY earnings growth, 12% production increase, and stable utility segment performance. Stock outperformed S&P 500 by 5.11 percentage points over the past year. Institutional investor (GAMCO) added to position despite market volatility, signaling confidence. Vertically integrated business model provides resilience and stable cash flows with 2.50% dividend yield.

title: National Fuel Expands In Ohio With $2.6 Billion CenterPoint Energy Deal
author: Lekha Gupta
description: National Fuel Gas Company is acquiring CenterPoint Energy's Ohio natural gas local distribution company for $2.62 billion, expanding its utility operations to serve approximately 1.1 million customers across three states.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Expanding utility operations, doubling rate base, and expecting immediate earnings accretion from the acquisition
Strategically divesting Ohio assets to recycle capital and refocus on core electric and natural gas operations
Mentioned as an investment vehicle related to the companies involved, with no specific positive or negative implications
